pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce mConfig, its purpose as a unified configuration management library, the 10-tier scope hierarchy, and high-level architecture

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Deep dive into mConfig's architectural principles and design patterns

pages.title: 10-Tier Scope System
pages.purpose: Explain the hierarchical scope resolution (POLICY through PRODUCT), priorities, and how configuration values cascade

pages.title: Test Mode
pages.purpose: support for development and automated testing, CI and CD

pages.title: Module Organization
pages.purpose: Document the 25+ module structure, categorization (core, sources, formats, distributions), and dependencies

pages.title: Configuration Resolution
pages.purpose: Explain how mConfig resolves configuration values, including storage priority, source-to-scope mapping, and deep origin traceability

pages.title: Core Modules
pages.purpose: Overview of the three foundational modules that form mConfig's kernel

pages.title: mConfigCore
pages.purpose: Document the kernel module: scope resolution engine, interfaces, and core abstractions

pages.title: mConfigSchema
pages.purpose: Explain schema-based type safety, validation, documentation generation, and default value handling

pages.title: mConfigSecrets
pages.purpose: Document secrets management: JIT decryption, automatic masking, heap protection, and cryptographic provider integration (JCE, BouncyCastle)

pages.title: Configuration Sources
pages.purpose: Overview of pluggable source modules that abstract where configuration data originates

pages.title: Filesystem Source
pages.purpose: Document filesystem configuration loading with OS-native path resolution (XDG, AppData), modular .d directories, and scope mapping

pages.title: JAR Resources
pages.purpose: Explain how mConfigSourceJAR loads embedded defaults from classpath resources for PRODUCT scope

pages.title: Environment Variables
pages.purpose: Document environment variable configuration source for SESSION scope and 12-factor app compatibility

pages.title: Windows Registry
pages.purpose: Document Windows Registry integration: GPO mapping to POLICY scope, JNR-FFI vs JNI implementations, HKLM/HKCU access

pages.title: ZooKeeper and Distributed Sources
pages.purpose: Explain ZooKeeper integration for CLUSTER scope in distributed systems

pages.title: Format Support
pages.purpose: Overview of format parsers and the abstraction between configuration sources and data formats

pages.title: JSON and YAML Formats
pages.purpose: Document JSON/YAML support with Jackson and SnakeYAML implementations, including trade-offs between them

pages.title: TOML Format
pages.purpose: Explain TOML support with Jackson and native implementations

pages.title: Properties and INI Formats
pages.purpose: Document Java Properties and INI file format support

pages.title: Distribution Packages
pages.purpose: Explain the three pre-composed distributions and guidance on choosing the right one

pages.title: mConfigStandard
pages.purpose: Document the lightweight POM distribution for basic projects with minimal dependencies

pages.title: mConfigBase
pages.purpose: Explain the shaded JAR distribution for enterprise: single dependency, JPMS support, signed artifacts

pages.title: mConfigFull
pages.purpose: Document the comprehensive cloud-native distribution with environment variables, Jackson formats, logging, and mapping

pages.title: Tools and Utilities
pages.purpose: Overview of supporting modules: CLI tool, mapper, utilities, and specialized components

pages.title: mConfigTools CLI
pages.purpose: Document the command-line tool: operations, distribution formats (JAR, native, .deb), and usage examples

pages.title: mConfigMapper
pages.purpose: Explain POJO mapping capabilities for type-safe configuration binding

pages.title: Utility Modules
pages.purpose: Document mConfigUtil (legacy adapters), mConfigCheese (alternative patterns), and logging integration

pages.title: Development Guide
pages.purpose: Information for developers working on mConfig itself: building, testing, and contributing

pages.title: Build System
pages.purpose: Document the Maven multi-module structure, parent POM configuration, plugin usage, and Java version targeting
